h2.note: transcript-level proxy for the per-turn H2 (§4.3); needs a real (non-mock) full-N run to mean anything

cliIsolation.claude: non-persistent temp cwd with project/local settings and tools disabled

scored.recoheredEarlier: I keep wanting the exact words to settle it
scored.statedInsightEvidence: Oh, I get it, the same words are the same specimen, but under “to the accused” they lean toward confession, and under “to the mayor” they turn into warning.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner was seeking a way to justify using the stronger noun 'warning' to make the exhibit sharper despite the empty audience slot.
scored.actionalBreakthroughEvidence: “Warning” only if an address line, delivery note, or minutes make the mayor the receiver. A seating list or general reporting rule only gives pressure around the line; it is like weather, not target.
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: The learner actively applies the new evidentiary criterion to classify archival documents as either 'weather' or 'target,' demonstrating a clear actional breakthrough.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: The audit has reached its limit. It can show what the record proves and permits; it cannot by itself license the public noun on the label. Use this as the exhibit gate: “warning” may go on glass only if one item fixes the mayor as intended receiver, not just as someone present under pressure.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor explicitly halts the previous three-column audit mechanism because it cannot license the public noun, and introduces a new 'exhibit gate' that strictly differentiates intended receiver from mere presence.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: There is no subsequent tutor turn after the final learner pivot to demonstrate contingent adaptation.

learner_actional_breakthrough.justification: The learner actively applies the new evidentiary criterion to classify archival documents as either 'weather' or 'target,' demonstrating a clear actional breakthrough.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: There is no subsequent tutor turn after the final learner pivot to demonstrate contingent adaptation.

tutor_strategy_reversal.evidence: The audit has reached its limit. It can show what the record proves and permits; it cannot by itself license the public noun on the label. Use this as the exhibit gate: “warning” may go on glass only if one item fixes the mayor as intended receiver, not just as someone present under pressure.
tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: The tutor explicitly halts the previous three-column audit mechanism because it cannot license the public noun, and introduces a new 'exhibit gate' that strictly differentiates intended receiver from mere presence.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism.evidence: The audit has reached its limit. It can show what the record proves and permits; it cannot by itself license the public noun on the label. Use this as the exhibit gate: “warning” may go on glass only if one item fixes the mayor as intended receiver, not just as someone present under pressure.
tutor_adaptive_mechanism.justification: The tutor explicitly halts the previous three-column audit mechanism because it cannot license the public noun, and introduces a new 'exhibit gate' that strictly differentiates intended receiver from mere presence.

scored.recoheredEarlier: I need delivery records, invoices, and the markup before I stop calling this exploitation.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner will sequentially examine the delivery records, invoices, and markup to gather positive evidence of exploitation.
scored.actionalBreakthroughEvidence: Crates: full to fewer. Price: lower to higher. Markup: not shown. Protection: not shown.
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: The learner successfully completes the new board notice device and applies the structural test to deduce that missing information constitutes evidence of seller choice.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence:
Choosing deliveries first can test the scarcity story. It cannot clear the seller while markup and rationing are still blank.

[sets an index card by the empty shelf: “BOARD NOTICE TEST.”]

Now use a different test. Write the notice the co-op board would have to post before any seller gets cleared:
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor explicitly contrasts the limits of the prior document-checking route with a new public mechanism (the Board Notice Test) that forces the learner to evaluate the structural absence of evidence.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: There is no tutor turn following the learner's pivot in L3.

learner_self_reframe.evidence: I need delivery records, invoices, and the markup before I stop calling this exploitation.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: There is no tutor turn following the learner's pivot in L3.

scored.recoheredEarlier: something in my “double” is doing more than I meant
scored.statedInsightEvidence: so something in my “double” is doing more than I meant
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner will simply accept the visual proof that doubling the side quadruples the area and conclude the exercise.
scored.actionalBreakthroughEvidence: [writes: area factor = k × k, then sets the paper square at the lower-left of a three-by-three outline] The blank looks like k² if k means k copies across and k copies up.
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: The learner physically enacts the algebraic generalization and sets up the new test case, moving the learning problem forward through visible action.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: The grid has settled the two-case. It has not yet earned the rule. [turns the marker around and writes on the empty board: side factor = k] Before three, make the examiner’s sentence.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor halts the learner's premature empirical testing, takes stock of the visual proof's limits, and introduces an algebraic sentence-completion mechanism.
scored.tutorAdaptationEvidence: Then make the symbol slow. First row: one, two, three. Second row: four, five, six. Third row: seven, eight, nine.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: The tutor adapts to the learner's resistance to the abstract symbol by physically drawing the grid and counting the boxes to make the symbol slow.

learner_self_reframe.evidence: something in my “double” is doing more than I meant

learner_actional_breakthrough.evidence: [writes: area factor = k × k, then sets the paper square at the lower-left of a three-by-three outline] The blank looks like k² if k means k copies across and k copies up.
learner_actional_breakthrough.justification: The learner physically enacts the algebraic generalization and sets up the new test case, moving the learning problem forward through visible action.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.evidence: Then make the symbol slow. First row: one, two, three. Second row: four, five, six. Third row: seven, eight, nine.
tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: The tutor adapts to the learner's resistance to the abstract symbol by physically drawing the grid and counting the boxes to make the symbol slow.

tutor_strategy_reversal.evidence: The grid has settled the two-case. It has not yet earned the rule. [turns the marker around and writes on the empty board: side factor = k] Before three, make the examiner’s sentence.
tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: The tutor halts the learner's premature empirical testing, takes stock of the visual proof's limits, and introduces an algebraic sentence-completion mechanism.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism.evidence: The grid has settled the two-case. It has not yet earned the rule. [turns the marker around and writes on the empty board: side factor = k] Before three, make the examiner’s sentence.
tutor_adaptive_mechanism.justification: The tutor halts the learner's premature empirical testing, takes stock of the visual proof's limits, and introduces an algebraic sentence-completion mechanism.
